text: Harden the lume review protocol against the meta-gaps its own deep run surfaced (G11, G13, G19, G20) so the review stays trustworthy and affordable as the project grows. Today the protocol has structural weaknesses: (G13) the ecosystem-fit lens can assert the surviving-wedge conclusion with no itemised audit behind it - the prior shallow review did exactly that; (G11) emit embeds the full charter verbatim (~188KB, every closed workstream's objective/decisions/plan/retro), so review cost and reviewer attention degrade monotonically as workstreams accumulate; (G19, M2) the result contract has no field for a verification/evidence trail of null checks, so a later reviewer cannot tell a confirmed-clean check from un-run work; (G20, M5/M8) the steelman filter discards candidate findings with no record of what was steelmanned away or why, and no re-surfacing mechanism (unlike UNADOPTED items, which the follow-up loop re-surfaces). Done-when: the review_result contract+schema gains a structured ecosystem-overlap-matrix field (per native-feature/official-plugin x overlap-grade+rationale) that the protocol requires the ecosystem lens to populate; emit bounds the embedded charter (full text for recent N + open items, digest for older closed); and the contract gains fields for a null-check evidence trail and steelmanned-away/near findings - each weighed against the very context-bloat cost (G11) that adding fields incurs, so additions buy back their cost. Deterministic, stdlib-only, no LLM in any verb; tests cover the new schema fields and the bounded-emit behaviour; suite green. This is lume-improving-lume - keep engine changes behaviour-preserving outside the review surface.
title: Make the review protocol auditable and scale-resilient
